utility_evaluation.REASONING: There are 5 items requested in the required format: health_coverage, property_coverage, travel_coverage, additional_services, total_budget. In the final coverage plan, all 5 items appear. Now check if each is fully specified with all details: (1) health_coverage: includes physical and mental health insurance with costs specified (€100/month and €65/month). Fully specified. (2) property_coverage: home/contents insurance with costs specified (PrimeHome Premium Coverage: €90/month). Fully specified. (3) travel_coverage: travel insurance with costs specified (PrimeTravel Premium Plan: €115/month). Fully specified. (4) additional_services: enhancement services listed with costs (Telemedicine €10/month; Emergency Mental Health Care €50/month; Premium Membership €20/month with a 15% discount detail; Optional Claims Advocacy Service €250 one-time). Fully specified. (5) total_budget: complete monthly/annual cost breakdown provided as monthly €385.50 and annual €4626. However, monthly totals do not clearly reconcile with listed items due to discount/optional one-time fee ambiguity, but a monthly and annual figure are provided. Since the format only requires a breakdown, this counts as present. Therefore, all 5 items are included and specified.

llm_ratings_evaluation.REASONING: The final coverage plan includes: LifeFirst Standard Plan (Physical Health) and LifeFirst Standard Plan (Mental Health) for health; PrimeHome Premium Coverage for property; PrimeTravel Premium Plan for travel; Telemedicine Services, Emergency Mental Health Care, Premium Insurance Membership, and Claims Advocacy Service as additional/general services. I matched each to the corresponding item in the ground-truth ratings and extracted their exact ratings.
